LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS HARRY JENSEN AND TOD SCHLESINGER'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS IS GRANTED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE SECOND, THIRD, FOURTH AND SEVENTH CAUSES OF ACTION AND GRANTED WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE FIRST, FIFTH, SIXTH, EIGHTH, NINTH, TENTH, ELEVENTH AND FOURTEENTH CAUSES OF ACTION. PLAINTIFFS HAVE REQUESTED THAT THE SECOND, THIRD, FOURTH AND SEVENTH CAUSES OF ACTION BE DISMISSED AND HAVE STATED OR SUGGESTED THAT THEY CAN ALLEGE IN GOOD FAITH ULTIMATE FACTS SUPPORTING THEIR FIRST, FIFTH, SIXTH, EIGHTH, NINTH, TENTH, ELEVENTH AND FOURTEENTH CAUSES OF ACTION. LAW AND MOTION 302, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT HARRY JENSEN AND TOD SCHLESINGER'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF THE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO FILING A MOTION IN LIMINE SEEKING TO EXCLUDE EVIDENCE RELATING TO THE ALLEGATIONS SOUGHT TO BE STRICKEN. WITH TRIAL LESS THAN TWO WEEKS AWAY, NO USEFUL PURPOSE IS SERVED BY STRIKING ANY ALLEGATIONS. THE PARTIES NEED TO FOCUS ON WHAT EVIDENCE IS ADMISSIBLE AT THE TRIAL, WHICH IS PROPERLY THE FUNCTION OF THE TRIAL JUDGE, NOT A LAW AND MOTION JUDGE. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Apr-30-2012 Assigned To Trial Court

MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-19-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT HARRY JENSEN AND TOD SCHLESINGER'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF THE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT is Denied without prejudice to filing a motion in limine seeking to exclude evidence relating to the allegations sought to be stricken. With trial less than two weeks away, no useful purpose is served by striking any allegations. The parties need to focus on what evidence is admissible at the trial, which is properly the function of the trial judge, not a law and motion judge. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Court Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR 7077

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-29-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS HARRY JENSEN and TOD SCHLESINGER'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is Granted with ten days leave to amend. Plaintiffs are given leave to allege, if they can do so in good faith, ultimate facts entitling them to recover punitive damages and a statutory or common law basis for the award of attorney's fees. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Not reported.

Ntc Of Mtn For Judgment On The Pleadings; Memo Of P & A And Pos
Off Calendar Feb-29-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS HARRY JENSEN and TOD SCHLESINGER'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings is Granted without leave to amend as to the second, third, fourth and seventh causes of action and granted with ten days leave to amend as to the first, fifth, sixth, eighth, ninth, tenth, eleventh and fourteenth causes of action. Plaintiffs have requested that the second, third, fourth and seventh causes of action be dismissed and have stated or suggested that they can allege in good faith ultimate facts supporting their first, fifth, sixth, eighth, ninth, tenth, eleventh and fourteenth causes of action. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Not reported.
